meaning: n.姣涘埄(Maoli,婢虫床鍦熻憲)鏃忕殑鎴樿垶
examples: Are you performing a haka, or just shuffling your feet?
Haka is a war-cry performed before a war or, nowadays, a rugby match.
Nowadays the haka is still led by a player of maori descent whenever and wherever the all blacks take the field.
Though viewers in Sydney and Brisbane watched the haka, he was faced with Julie Andrews singing Do-Re-Mi.
Rhyl and Carmarthen face a similar disadvantage as Haka and Brann are also well into their league campaigns.
